title: Analysis
text:
Analysis of Forest Fires in Turkey
This page presents the exploratory data analysis of forest fire statistics in Turkey between 1988 and 2024.


Key Takeaways

Forest fires in Turkey fluctuate significantly over time.
Some years experienced extremely high burned forest areas.
Burned areas and fire frequency do not always increase together.
Negligence-related fires constitute a major proportion of forest fire causes.
Forest fires remain an important environmental issue in Turkey.



Load Libraries


Code
library(tidyverse)


── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
✔ dplyr     1.2.0     ✔ readr     2.2.0
✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
✔ ggplot2   4.0.2     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
✔ lubridate 1.9.5     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
✔ purrr     1.2.2     
── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors


Code
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(janitor)



Attaching package: 'janitor'

The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':

    chisq.test, fisher.test




Import and Clean Dataset


Code
fires <- read_excel("forest_fires.xlsx") %>%
  clean_names() %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), ~na_if(as.character(.), "-"))) %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), ~as.numeric(gsub(",", ".", .))))

glimpse(fires)


Rows: 37
Columns: 11
$ year                <dbl> 1988, 1989, 1990, 1991, 1992, 1993, 1994, 1995, 19…
$ burned_area         <dbl> 18210.0, 13099.0, 13742.0, 8081.0, 12232.0, 15393.…
$ number_of_fires     <dbl> 1372, 1633, 1750, 1481, 2117, 2545, 3239, 1770, 16…
$ intentional_number  <dbl> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, 193, 249, 279,…
$ intentional_hectare <dbl> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, 923.00, 1655.0…
$ negligence_number   <dbl> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, 696, 1163, 115…
$ negligence_hectare  <dbl> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, 3389, 3713, 28…
$ natural_number      <dbl> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, 78, 53, 203, 1…
$ natural_hecatre     <dbl> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, 37.0, 20.0, 12…
$ unknown_number      <dbl> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, 372, 467, 442,…
$ unknown_hectare     <dbl> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, 1968.0, 1376.0…


Code
summary(fires)


      year       burned_area     number_of_fires intentional_number

 3rd Qu.:2015   3rd Qu.: 14922   3rd Qu.:2579    3rd Qu.:252.8     
 Max.   :2024   Max.   :139503   Max.   :3797    Max.   :410.0     
                                                 NA's   :9         
 intentional_hectare negligence_number negligence_hectare natural_number 
 Min.   :   85.0     Min.   : 693.0    Min.   : 1198      Min.   : 53.0  
 1st Qu.:  372.0     1st Qu.: 853.2    1st Qu.: 3014      1st Qu.:170.8  
 Median :  675.5     Median :1009.5    Median : 4871      Median :295.5  
 Mean   : 2632.7     Mean   :1073.3    Mean   : 7971      Mean   :276.9

 NA's   :9           NA's   :9         NA's   :9          NA's   :9      
 natural_hecatre  unknown_number   unknown_hectare  
 Min.   : 20.00   Min.   : 251.0   Min.   :  287.8

 Mean   :266.11   Mean   : 802.9   Mean   : 4091.5  
 3rd Qu.:367.00   3rd Qu.: 997.8   3rd Qu.: 3305.8  
 Max.   :735.00   Max.   :1859.0   Max.   :46269.0  
 NA's   :9        NA's   :9        NA's   :9        




Number of Forest Fires by Year


Code
ggplot(fires, aes(x = year, y = number_of_fires)) +
  geom_line(color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(color = "darkred", size = 2) +
  labs(
    title = "Number of Forest Fires by Year",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Number of Fires"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()









The number of forest fires fluctuates considerably across years.


Burned Forest Area by Year


Code
ggplot(fires, aes(x = year, y = burned_area)) +
  geom_line(color = "darkgreen", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(color = "forestgreen", size = 2) +
  labs(
    title = "Burned Forest Area by Year",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Burned Area (Hectares)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()









The burned forest area changes significantly across years. The year 2021 stands out as an extreme year.


Relationship Between Fire Numbers and Burned Area


Code
ggplot(fires, aes(x = number_of_fires, y = burned_area)) +
  geom_point(color = "orange", size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "Relationship Between Fire Numbers and Burned Area",
    x = "Number of Fires",
    y = "Burned Area"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()









The relationship between the number of fires and burned area is not perfectly linear.


Fire Causes Analysis


Code
causes <- fires %>%
  summarise(
    Intentional = sum(intentional_number, na.rm = TRUE),
    Negligence = sum(negligence_number, na.rm = TRUE),
    Natural = sum(natural_number, na.rm = TRUE),
    Unknown = sum(unknown_number, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = everything(),
    names_to = "Cause",
    values_to = "Count"
  )

ggplot(causes, aes(x = reorder(Cause, Count), y = Count, fill = Cause)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Total Forest Fires by Cause",
    x = "Cause",
    y = "Total Number of Fires"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()









Negligence-related fires represent one of the largest categories among forest fire causes.


Burned Area by Fire Cause


Code
hectares <- fires %>%
  summarise(
    Intentional = sum(intentional_hectare, na.rm = TRUE),
    Negligence = sum(negligence_hectare, na.rm = TRUE),
    Natural = sum(natural_hecatre, na.rm = TRUE),
    Unknown = sum(unknown_hectare, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = everything(),
    names_to = "Cause",
    values_to = "Hectares"
  )

ggplot(hectares, aes(x = reorder(Cause, Hectares), y = Hectares, fill = Cause)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Burned Area by Fire Cause",
    x = "Cause",
    y = "Burned Area"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()









Negligence-related fires account for a substantial proportion of total burned forest areas.


Top 10 Years with Highest Burned Area


Code
fires %>%
  arrange(desc(burned_area)) %>%
  slice(1:10) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(as.factor(year), burned_area), y = burned_area)) +
  geom_col(fill = "firebrick") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Top 10 Years with Highest Burned Area",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Burned Area"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()









2021 appears as the most destructive year in terms of burned forest area.


Conclusion
Forest fires continue to be an important environmental issue in Turkey. The analysis shows that both fire frequency and burned area vary considerably over time. Human-related causes, especially negligence, play an important role in forest fire occurrence.

title: Data
text:
Data


Data Source
The dataset used in this project was obtained from the official statistics published by the General Directorate of Forestry (OGM), the official institution responsible for forest management and forest fire monitoring in Turkey.
The official data source can be accessed from the following link:
OGM Official Statistics


About the Dataset
This dataset contains yearly forest fire statistics in Turkey between 1988 and 2024.
The dataset includes the following variables:

Year: Year of observation
Burned_area: Total burned forest area in hectares
Number_of_fires: Total number of forest fires
Intentional_number: Number of intentional fires
Intentional_hectare: Burned area caused by intentional fires
Negligence_number: Number of negligence-caused fires
Negligence_hectare: Burned area caused by negligence
Natural_number: Number of natural fires
Natural_hecatre: Burned area caused by natural fires
Unknown_number: Number of fires with unknown causes
Unknown_hectare: Burned area caused by unknown reasons

The data was selected because forest fires are an important environmental and socio-economic issue in Turkey. Analyzing these statistics helps us understand long-term trends, fire severity, and the role of different fire causes.


Importing the Dataset


Code
library(tidyverse)


── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
✔ dplyr     1.2.0     ✔ readr     2.2.0
✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
✔ ggplot2   4.0.2     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
✔ lubridate 1.9.5     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
✔ purrr     1.2.2     
── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors


Code
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(janitor)



Attaching package: 'janitor'

The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':

    chisq.test, fisher.test


Code
fires <- read_excel("forest_fires.xlsx") %>%
  clean_names()

head(fires)


# A tibble: 6 × 11
   year burned_area number_of_fires intentional_number intentional_hectare
  <dbl>       <dbl>           <dbl> <chr>              <chr>              
1  1988       18210            1372 -                  -                  
2  1989       13099            1633 -                  -                  
3  1990       13742            1750 -                  -                  
4  1991        8081            1481 -                  -                  
5  1992       12232            2117 -                  -                  
6  1993       15393            2545 -                  -                  
# ℹ 6 more variables: negligence_number <chr>, negligence_hectare <chr>,
#   natural_number <chr>, natural_hecatre <chr>, unknown_number <chr>,
#   unknown_hectare <chr>




Data Preparation
The original dataset contains some missing values represented with -, especially for fire cause variables before 1997. These values were converted into NA.
Additionally, column names were standardized and numeric values were converted into a suitable format for analysis.


Code
fires_clean <- fires %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), ~na_if(as.character(.), "-"))) %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), ~as.numeric(gsub(",", ".", .))))

glimpse(fires_clean)


Rows: 37
Columns: 11
$ year                <dbl> 1988, 1989, 1990, 1991, 1992, 1993, 1994, 1995, 19…
$ burned_area         <dbl> 18210.0, 13099.0, 13742.0, 8081.0, 12232.0, 15393.…
$ number_of_fires     <dbl> 1372, 1633, 1750, 1481, 2117, 2545, 3239, 1770, 16…
$ intentional_number  <dbl> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, 193, 249, 279,…
$ intentional_hectare <dbl> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, 923.00, 1655.0…
$ negligence_number   <dbl> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, 696, 1163, 115…
$ negligence_hectare  <dbl> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, 3389, 3713, 28…
$ natural_number      <dbl> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, 78, 53, 203, 1…
$ natural_hecatre     <dbl> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, 37.0, 20.0, 12…
$ unknown_number      <dbl> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, 372, 467, 442,…
$ unknown_hectare     <dbl> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, 1968.0, 1376.0…

Final Dataset
The cleaned dataset was saved in .RData format for reproducibility.


Code
save(fires_clean, file = "final_forest_fires.RData")


The final cleaned dataset can be downloaded here:
Download Final Dataset

text:
1. Temporal Analysis (Time Series)

The number of fires and area burned will be examined by year
Increasing/decreasing trends will be analyzed

title: DataFlame
text:

Fire distribution by region will be compared
Risky regions will be identified
